description:
A storage administrator would like a quick, reproducible snapshot of the current write-performance of the workstation’s primary filesystem and of the free space that is still available afterwards.

  1. Create the directory  
     /home/user/benchmark  
     if it does **not** already exist.

  2. Run a single buffered write-test that writes exactly 1 GiB of zeros to a file called  
     /home/user/benchmark/perf_test.img  
     using a block-size of 16 MiB.  
     • The command you choose must force the data to be flushed to disk before it finishes so that a real physical write is measured.  
     • Capture the numeric throughput value (in MB/s) that the tool prints to STDOUT.  
       – The throughput must be rounded to two decimal places in the final report.  

  3. Immediately determine the free space still available on the filesystem that holds /home/user (report it in megabytes, **integer MB**).

  4. Produce the log file  
     /home/user/benchmark/disk_speed.log  
     with **exactly five non-empty lines**, each of them a simple key=value pair as shown below.  
     • The file **must not** contain additional spaces, tabs or blank lines.  
     • Keys must appear **in the exact order** given here:

        DATE=<ISO-8601 local time stamp to the second, e.g. 2025-03-14 15:09:26>  
        BYTES_WRITTEN=1073741824      ← always 1 GiB in bytes  
        BLOCK_SIZE=16777216           ← always 16 MiB in bytes  
        WRITE_SPEED_MB_PER_SEC=<floating-point number with exactly two decimals>  
        DISK_FREE_MB=<integer number of megabytes still free after the test>

     Example of a syntactically valid file (values will differ on your system):

        DATE=2025-03-14 15:09:26  
        BYTES_WRITTEN=1073741824  
        BLOCK_SIZE=16777216  
        WRITE_SPEED_MB_PER_SEC=215.47  
        DISK_FREE_MB=248352

  5. Leave the test file (/home/user/benchmark/perf_test.img) in place; it will be removed by the grader.

  After completing the steps, you should be able to display the log with  
     cat /home/user/benchmark/disk_speed.log  
  and it should match the exact 5-line, key=value format described above.
truth:
<!--
     The grader will verify:

     1. Directory /home/user/benchmark exists.

     2. File /home/user/benchmark/perf_test.img exists and its size is
        exactly 1073741824 bytes (1 GiB).

     3. File /home/user/benchmark/disk_speed.log exists, is readable and
        contains exactly 5 lines (line count == 5).

     4. The content of /home/user/benchmark/disk_speed.log must match:

        ^DATE=\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}\n
        BYTES_WRITTEN=1073741824\n
        BLOCK_SIZE=16777216\n
        WRITE_SPEED_MB_PER_SEC=\d+\.\d{2}\n
        DISK_FREE_MB=\d+\n$

        (One trailing newline is allowed.)

     5. No other files are required, and permissions must allow
        the non-root user to read both files (0644 or more permissive).
  -->
name: task_000000_355b04a3
